title: Pouteria laeteviridis
taxonomy: Pouteria laeteviridis is classified in the family Sapotaceae, a family of flowering plants that includes over 900 species. This family is part of the order Ebenales, which is a group of flowering plants. The Sapotaceae family is native to tropical regions of the world. Pouteria laeteviridis is a species of flowering plant that is native to New Caledonia. It is a member of the genus Pouteria, which contains species of trees and shrubs.
uses: Pouteria laeteviridis has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its bark and leaves are reportedly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. In traditional medicine, it is also used as a treatment for infections and skin conditions. The plant's antimicrobial properties are thought to contribute to its medicinal effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal uses.

It contains compounds with antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its medicinal value. preparations: Pouteria laeteviridis, also known as the yellow-green pouteria, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Pouteria laeteviridis, also known as the Yellow Green Sapote, is a species of evergreen tree native to Central and South America. The health benefits of Pouteria laeteviridis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
